project.name: Module 3: Storage Service
project.description: Create lib/storage.ts — a thin wrapper around browser.storage.local (via WXT's cross-browser storage API) that encrypts data before saving and decrypts after loading. Uses encrypt/decrypt from lib/crypto.ts (Module 2). The storage key 'holyPrivateData' is preserved for backward compatibility with Holy PB. All functions return Result<T, E> types — no exceptions for expected errors. Target: ~100-150 lines.
project.revision_notes: v1.1.0 — 7 fixes from Claude Code pre-implementation review: (1) Added reason field to StorageErrorContext, (2) Fixed loadEncryptedData return type to union StorageError | InvalidPasswordError, (3) Promoted function signatures to acceptance criteria, (4) Made validateEncryptedStore a public export, (5) Added progress_log.format_rules, (6) Fixed test count, command conventions, and named magic constants, (7) Added prerequisite step to extend StorageErrorContext in errors.ts.
project.parent_plan: Hush Consolidated Build Plan — Day 2 (first third)

preserve_verbatim: The storage key 'holyPrivateData' used for the single encrypted blob in browser.storage.local — this is the key Holy PB uses and maintaining it allows potential future migration paths

The encrypt-entire-tree-as-single-blob pattern — Holy PB stores the complete bookmark tree as one encrypted string under one key. This is simple, atomic, and avoids partial-write corruption. Hush preserves this pattern.
The flow: serialize BookmarkTree to JSON string → encrypt string with user password → store encrypted blob under 'holyPrivateData'. Reverse for load.

falls_short.deficiency: Holy PB's saveEncrypted/loadEncrypted in shared.js have zero error handling — chrome.runtime.lastError is never checked, storage failures are silent
falls_short.hush_requires: Every storage operation returns Result<T, StorageError>. StorageError includes typed context: { key, operation, reason } where reason is a discriminated union. No silent failures.

falls_short.deficiency: Holy PB has no retry logic — if a transient storage write fails (e.g., browser is busy), the data is lost
falls_short.hush_requires: Exponential backoff retry for transient failures (read/write errors). Configurable via RETRY_CONFIG constant. NOT retried: permanent errors (quota exceeded, corrupted data, wrong password).

falls_short.deficiency: Holy PB cannot distinguish between 'no data saved yet' (first run) and 'data is corrupted' — both return undefined from loadEncrypted
falls_short.hush_requires: loadEncryptedData returns distinct error reasons: 'not_found' (no data under key — first run), 'corrupted' (data exists but fails JSON parse or EncryptedStore validation), 'read_failed' (browser storage API error).

falls_short.deficiency: Holy PB uses raw chrome.storage.local which only works in Chrome — no Firefox/Edge compatibility
falls_short.hush_requires: Use WXT's browser.storage.local (auto-polyfilled via webextension-polyfill). Import from 'wxt/browser' not 'chrome'.

falls_short.deficiency: Holy PB has no concept of storage quota — if the encrypted blob exceeds Chrome's 10MB default limit (or 5MB per-item QUOTA_BYTES_PER_ITEM), the write silently fails
falls_short.hush_requires: Quota awareness: check estimated size before write. DEFAULT_STORAGE_QUOTA constant (10MB). QuotaExceededError detection. getStorageUsage() utility. Note: extensions can request 'unlimitedStorage' permission to bypass this limit — not needed for v1.0 but the architecture should not hardcode the 10MB assumption.

do_not_incorporate: Holy PB's window.HolyShared global attachment pattern — use ES module exports
Holy PB's direct UI coupling in shared.js (the same file that does storage also manipulates DOM) — lib/storage.ts is pure storage, zero UI
chrome.storage.session usage — not needed for persistent encrypted bookmark storage
build_from_scratch: saveEncryptedData(plaintext: string, password: string): Promise<Result<void, StorageError>> — encrypt + save with error handling
loadEncryptedData(password: string): Promise<Result<string, StorageError | InvalidPasswordError>> — load + decrypt with typed error reasons including wrong password
hasData(): Promise<Result<boolean, StorageError>> — check if encrypted data exists without decrypting
clearAll(): Promise<Result<void, StorageError>> — remove all Hush data from storage
getStorageUsage(): Promise<Result<{ used: number; quota: number }, StorageError>> — storage quota info
validateEncryptedStore(data: unknown): data is EncryptedStore — type guard for stored data validation (public export, used by Module 5 backup import and Module 14 migration)
Exponential backoff retry wrapper with RETRY_CONFIG constant (maxAttempts: 3, delays: [100, 200, 400])

user_stories.title: Encrypted save and load roundtrip
user_stories.description: Create lib/storage.ts with saveEncryptedData() and loadEncryptedData(). Save encrypts plaintext with the user's password (via lib/crypto.ts encrypt()) and stores the resulting EncryptedStore JSON under the key 'holyPrivateData' in browser.storage.local. Load retrieves the blob, validates it with validateEncryptedStore(), and decrypts it back to plaintext.
steps: PREREQUISITE: Extend StorageErrorContext in lib/errors.ts to add reason field: reason?: 'not_found' | 'corrupted' | 'quota_exceeded' | 'write_failed' | 'read_failed'. This is required before any storage tests can reference error reasons.

RED: Write tests/unit/lib/storage.test.ts. Mock browser.storage.local (WXT provides this via wxt/testing or manual mock). Write test: 'saveEncryptedData stores encrypted data under holyPrivateData key'
RED: Write test: 'loadEncryptedData retrieves and decrypts data stored by saveEncryptedData' — save then load with same password, assert plaintext matches
RED: Write test: 'saveEncryptedData stores valid EncryptedStore JSON' — save, read raw storage, parse JSON, assert it has salt/encrypted/iv/iterations fields
RED: Write test: 'loadEncryptedData returns not_found error when no data exists' — fresh storage, assert Result is failure with reason 'not_found'
RED: Write test: 'STORAGE_KEY constant equals holyPrivateData'

GREEN: Create lib/storage.ts. Import encrypt/decrypt from '@/lib/crypto'. Import browser from 'wxt/browser'. Define STORAGE_KEY = 'holyPrivateData'. Implement saveEncryptedData(plaintext: string, password: string): Promise<Result<void, StorageError>> and loadEncryptedData(password: string): Promise<Result<string, StorageError | InvalidPasswordError>>.
REFACTOR: Extract validateEncryptedStore(data: unknown): data is EncryptedStore type guard as a public export
acceptance_criteria: saveEncryptedData(plaintext: string, password: string): Promise<Result<void, StorageError>> — encrypts plaintext and stores under STORAGE_KEY
loadEncryptedData(password: string): Promise<Result<string, StorageError | InvalidPasswordError>> — loads, validates, and decrypts. Returns union error type (never throws for expected errors).
Save/load roundtrip preserves plaintext exactly
Data stored under STORAGE_KEY = 'holyPrivateData'
Stored value is valid EncryptedStore JSON (salt, encrypted, iv, iterations)
loadEncryptedData returns StorageError with reason 'not_found' when no data exists
validateEncryptedStore is a public export (type guard)
StorageErrorContext in lib/errors.ts has reason?: 'not_found' | 'corrupted' | 'quota_exceeded' | 'write_failed' | 'read_failed'
Uses browser.storage.local from 'wxt/browser' (not chrome.storage.local)
user_stories.verification: npx vitest run tests/unit/lib/storage.test.ts && npx tsc --noEmit

user_stories.title: Error handling for all failure modes
user_stories.description: Add comprehensive error handling to saveEncryptedData and loadEncryptedData. Every failure mode must produce a typed StorageError (or InvalidPasswordError) with a specific reason — never an unhandled exception for expected errors.
steps: RED: Write test: 'loadEncryptedData returns corrupted error for invalid JSON in storage' — store raw string 'not-json' under key, assert Result failure with reason 'corrupted'
RED: Write test: 'loadEncryptedData returns corrupted error for valid JSON but not EncryptedStore' — store '{}' under key, assert reason 'corrupted'

RED: Write test: 'loadEncryptedData returns InvalidPasswordError for wrong password' — save with password A, load with password B. Assert error is InvalidPasswordError (not StorageError). The return type Result<string, StorageError | InvalidPasswordError> allows caller to pattern-match on error type.
RED: Write test: 'saveEncryptedData returns quota_exceeded error when data exceeds quota' — mock storage.local.set to throw QuotaExceededError, assert reason 'quota_exceeded'
RED: Write test: 'saveEncryptedData returns write_failed error for generic storage failure' — mock storage.local.set to throw generic error, assert reason 'write_failed'
RED: Write test: 'loadEncryptedData returns read_failed error for generic storage failure' — mock storage.local.get to throw, assert reason 'read_failed'

GREEN: Add try/catch to saveEncryptedData and loadEncryptedData. Catch QuotaExceededError specifically. Catch InvalidPasswordError from decrypt() and return it directly in the Result. Wrap all other errors in StorageError with appropriate reason.
REFACTOR: Ensure all error paths set the reason field on StorageErrorContext
acceptance_criteria: Invalid JSON in storage → StorageError with reason 'corrupted'
Valid JSON but not EncryptedStore → StorageError with reason 'corrupted'
Wrong password → InvalidPasswordError (part of union return type, not thrown)
Storage quota exceeded → StorageError with reason 'quota_exceeded'
Generic write failure → StorageError with reason 'write_failed'
Generic read failure → StorageError with reason 'read_failed'
No error path throws — all return Result failures
user_stories.verification: npx vitest run tests/unit/lib/storage.test.ts && npx tsc --noEmit

user_stories.title: Retry logic with exponential backoff
user_stories.description: Add retry logic for transient storage failures. Uses exponential backoff defined in a RETRY_CONFIG constant. Only retries transient errors (read_failed, write_failed). Does NOT retry permanent errors (quota_exceeded, corrupted, not_found, InvalidPasswordError).
steps: RED: Write test: 'retries on transient read failure up to RETRY_CONFIG.maxAttempts times' — mock storage.local.get to fail twice then succeed, assert final result is success
RED: Write test: 'retries on transient write failure' — mock storage.local.set to fail once then succeed
RED: Write test: 'does NOT retry on quota_exceeded error' — mock quota error, assert only 1 attempt
RED: Write test: 'does NOT retry on InvalidPasswordError' — assert only 1 attempt
RED: Write test: 'returns last error after all retries exhausted' — mock 3 consecutive failures, assert Result is failure
RED: Write test: 'RETRY_CONFIG has maxAttempts: 3 and delays: [100, 200, 400]'

GREEN: Create withRetry() helper. Define RETRY_CONFIG = { maxAttempts: 3, delays: [100, 200, 400] } as a named constant. Wrap save/load in withRetry. Check error reason before retrying — only retry 'read_failed' and 'write_failed'.
REFACTOR: Ensure withRetry is internal (not exported) — it's an implementation detail
acceptance_criteria: Transient read/write failures are retried up to RETRY_CONFIG.maxAttempts (3) times
Exponential backoff delays defined in RETRY_CONFIG.delays: [100, 200, 400] ms
Permanent errors (quota_exceeded, corrupted, not_found) are NOT retried
InvalidPasswordError is NOT retried
After all retries exhausted, returns the last StorageError
RETRY_CONFIG is a named exported constant (for testing), not magic numbers
definition_of_done: No magic number delays in implementation
user_stories.verification: npx vitest run tests/unit/lib/storage.test.ts && npx tsc --noEmit

user_stories.title: Utility functions: hasData, clearAll, getStorageUsage
user_stories.description: Add three utility functions for storage management. hasData() checks if encrypted data exists without decrypting. clearAll() removes all Hush data. getStorageUsage() reports storage consumption against DEFAULT_STORAGE_QUOTA.
steps: RED: Write test: 'hasData returns true when data exists under STORAGE_KEY'
RED: Write test: 'hasData returns false when no data exists'
RED: Write test: 'clearAll removes data from storage'
RED: Write test: 'clearAll followed by hasData returns false'
RED: Write test: 'getStorageUsage returns { used: number, quota: number }' — assert quota equals DEFAULT_STORAGE_QUOTA
RED: Write test: 'DEFAULT_STORAGE_QUOTA equals 10_485_760 (10MB)'
GREEN: Implement hasData(), clearAll(), getStorageUsage(). Define DEFAULT_STORAGE_QUOTA = 10_485_760. Note in JSDoc that extensions can request unlimitedStorage permission to bypass this limit.
REFACTOR: Ensure all three return Result types
acceptance_criteria: hasData(): Promise<Result<boolean, StorageError>>
clearAll(): Promise<Result<void, StorageError>>
getStorageUsage(): Promise<Result<{ used: number; quota: number }, StorageError>>
DEFAULT_STORAGE_QUOTA = 10_485_760 (10MB) as named exported constant
JSDoc on DEFAULT_STORAGE_QUOTA notes unlimitedStorage permission option
All three functions use Result return types
user_stories.verification: npx vitest run tests/unit/lib/storage.test.ts && npx tsc --noEmit

user_stories.title: validateEncryptedStore type guard and module purity
user_stories.description: Verify validateEncryptedStore works as a public type guard. Verify module purity: lib/storage.ts must have zero React/DOM imports, only browser.storage.local from 'wxt/browser' as the sole browser API.
steps: RED: Write test: 'validateEncryptedStore returns true for valid EncryptedStore' — object with salt, encrypted, iv, iterations fields
RED: Write test: 'validateEncryptedStore returns false for null/undefined/string/number'
RED: Write test: 'validateEncryptedStore returns false for object missing required fields'
RED: Write test: 'validateEncryptedStore returns false for object with wrong field types'
RED: Write purity test: 'storage.ts has zero React/DOM imports' — read source, assert no react/ReactDOM imports
RED: Write purity test: 'storage.ts only imports browser from wxt/browser' — assert no chrome.* imports
GREEN: Ensure validateEncryptedStore checks all 4 required fields with correct types (string for salt/encrypted/iv, number for iterations)
REFACTOR: Tighten type guard if needed
acceptance_criteria: validateEncryptedStore is exported publicly from lib/storage.ts
Returns true only for objects with: salt (string), encrypted (string), iv (string), iterations (number)
Returns false for null, undefined, primitives, partial objects, wrong types
lib/storage.ts has zero React/DOM imports
lib/storage.ts uses 'wxt/browser' not 'chrome' for storage access

Verify full test suite: tsc + eslint + vitest + coverage + wxt build + npm run test:e2e

lib/storage.ts <= 150 lines
All functions <= 50 lines

what_exists.project_scaffold: Module 1 complete. WXT + React + TypeScript + Tailwind v4 + shadcn/ui + Vitest + Playwright + ESLint + Sentry. 203 unit tests, 10 E2E tests passing.
what_exists.crypto_module: Module 2 complete. lib/crypto.ts exports: encrypt(plaintext, password) -> Promise<EncryptedStore>, decrypt(store, password) -> Promise<string> (throws InvalidPasswordError on wrong password), deriveKey(password, salt) -> Promise<CryptoKey>, generateSalt() -> string, verifyPassword(store, password) -> Promise<boolean>, CRYPTO_CONFIG (algorithm: 'AES-GCM', keyLength: 256, iterations: 600000, hash: 'SHA-256'). 266+ tests passing.
what_exists.types: lib/types.ts defines: Bookmark, Folder, BookmarkNode, BookmarkTree, EncryptedStore { salt: string; encrypted: string; iv: string; iterations: number }, Result<T, E> = { success: true; data: T } | { success: false; error: E }
what_exists.errors: lib/errors.ts defines: StorageError extends Error with context: StorageErrorContext { key: string; operation: 'read' | 'write' | 'delete' }. InvalidPasswordError extends Error. NOTE: StorageErrorContext currently does NOT have a reason field — STORAGE-001 must add it as a prerequisite step.
what_exists.wxt_browser: WXT provides browser.storage.local via 'wxt/browser' import. This is a cross-browser polyfill for chrome.storage.local / browser.storage.local. API: browser.storage.local.get(key), browser.storage.local.set({key: value}), browser.storage.local.remove(key), browser.storage.local.getBytesInUse().

what_does_not_exist.storage_module: lib/storage.ts does not exist. No storage functionality exists yet.
what_does_not_exist.reason_field: StorageErrorContext does NOT have a 'reason' field yet — must be added in STORAGE-001 prerequisite step.

relevant_files.what_to_extract: saveEncrypted() and loadEncrypted() functions (~30 lines total). Search for 'holyPrivateData' in the IIFE to find the storage key and save/load logic.
relevant_files.extraction_notes: The save/load pattern is simple: JSON.stringify the tree, encrypt, store under key. Reverse for load. Extract the pattern, add error handling and Result types.

tech_stack.storage_api: browser.storage.local from 'wxt/browser'. This is WXT's cross-browser wrapper around chrome.storage.local / browser.storage.local. It returns Promises (no callback API).
tech_stack.testing: Vitest with jsdom environment. browser.storage.local must be mocked in tests. WXT may provide test utilities (check wxt/testing). If not, create a manual mock that implements get/set/remove/getBytesInUse.
forbidden_patterns: chrome.storage.session — not needed for persistent storage
Direct DOM manipulation — lib/storage.ts is pure storage logic
React imports — lib/ module purity rule
Throwing exceptions for expected errors — use Result<T, E> returns

error_model.description: Union Result type pattern. loadEncryptedData returns Result<string, StorageError | InvalidPasswordError>. Callers pattern-match on error type to distinguish storage failures from wrong password. This is consistent with the Result-everywhere approach and avoids mixed error models (Result + throw).
error_model.wrong_password_flow: decrypt() in lib/crypto.ts THROWS InvalidPasswordError. lib/storage.ts catches this and wraps it into the Result failure. The caller never sees a thrown exception from loadEncryptedData.
error_model.permanent_vs_transient: Permanent errors (not_found, corrupted, quota_exceeded, InvalidPasswordError) are returned immediately. Transient errors (read_failed, write_failed) are retried before returning.

licensing.storage_attribution: Storage pattern (encrypt-tree-as-blob under holyPrivateData key) derived from Holy Private Bookmarks shared.js (GPL-3.0).

inputs.modification_needed: Add reason field to StorageErrorContext: reason?: 'not_found' | 'corrupted' | 'quota_exceeded' | 'write_failed' | 'read_failed'

inputs.what_it_provides: encrypt(plaintext, password) -> Promise<EncryptedStore>, decrypt(store, password) -> Promise<string> (throws InvalidPasswordError)

outputs.consumed_by: Module 8 (Background Service Worker — auto-save), Module 10 (Popup UI — unlock/lock), Module 12 (Settings — clear data), Module 15a (Sync Client — read/write local store)
outputs.description: Storage service. ~100-150 lines. Public exports: saveEncryptedData, loadEncryptedData, hasData, clearAll, getStorageUsage, validateEncryptedStore, STORAGE_KEY, DEFAULT_STORAGE_QUOTA, RETRY_CONFIG.

outputs.consumed_by: All modules that handle storage errors
outputs.description: StorageErrorContext gains reason field for typed error discrimination.
~30 test cases covering: roundtrip, error handling, retry, utilities, validation, purity. 80%+ branch coverage.

known_risks.issue: browser.storage.local mocking in Vitest — WXT may or may not provide test utilities for storage
known_risks.action: Check if wxt/testing exports a storage mock. If not, create a minimal mock: { get: vi.fn(), set: vi.fn(), remove: vi.fn(), getBytesInUse: vi.fn() }. Mock at module level.

known_risks.issue: QuotaExceededError detection — Chrome throws DOMException with name 'QuotaExceededError', Firefox may throw differently
known_risks.action: Check error.name === 'QuotaExceededError' OR error.message includes 'quota'. WXT's polyfill may normalize this. Test with both patterns.

known_risks.issue: decrypt() throws InvalidPasswordError — must be caught and wrapped into Result, not allowed to propagate
known_risks.action: loadEncryptedData must have a try/catch specifically for decrypt(). If InvalidPasswordError, return { success: false, error: new InvalidPasswordError() }. This is the key architectural decision (union Result type).

known_risks.issue: StorageErrorContext modification — adding reason field to errors.ts could affect existing Module 1/2 tests if they assert on context shape
known_risks.action: reason is optional (?), so existing code won't break. Run full test suite after modification to confirm.

format_rules.format:
Append-only. Each entry: ## STORAGE-XXX — <title>
- Status: PASS | FAIL (attempt N)
- Tests: N passing, N failing
- Coverage: N% branches
- Notes: <any issues encountered>
- Timestamp: ISO 8601
format_rules.update_trigger: After each story completion or failure

metadata.session_protocol: One Claude Code session. Extend errors.ts first (add reason field). Then generate storage.ts + storage.test.ts. Mock browser.storage.local. Run tsc + eslint + vitest. Commit.
metadata.quality_gate: All storage tests pass. tsc clean. No empty catch blocks. lib/ coverage >= 80%. Line count <= 150. Zero browser/chrome API calls except browser.storage.local via wxt/browser.
metadata.human_review_required: Review error model: confirm loadEncryptedData returns Result<string, StorageError | InvalidPasswordError> (union type, not thrown). Verify retry logic only retries transient errors.

execution_rules.execution_order: Execute stories in dependency order: STORAGE-001 first (includes errors.ts prerequisite), then STORAGE-002, then STORAGE-003, then STORAGE-004 and STORAGE-005 (parallel), then STORAGE-006 as final validation.
execution_rules.clarifying_questions: You ARE encouraged to ask clarifying questions about intent or trade-offs. The PRD should contain all factual context needed. If you must ask a factual question, it indicates a PRD gap.
execution_rules.prd_scope: Module 3 of 22 in a sequential build plan.
execution_rules.progress_logging: After completing each story, update this prd.json (passes, attempt_count) AND append to progress.md per progress_log.format_rules.
execution_rules.commit_protocol: Commit after each story. Format: 'feat(storage): STORAGE-XXX — <title>'.
execution_rules.failure_protocol: Log failure in progress.md with root cause. Increment attempt_count. Max 3 attempts per story.
